abstract.text: Use Property Wrappers to validate a form.

inlineContent.text: To validate a property, you use @
attribute with a list of validations. Every validation has a
parameter.

code: @Validate(.min(16, message: "you must be at least 16 years old"))

inlineContent.text: Most of the time, a non-
value needs to be provided for the element being validated. That’s why the default value of age is set to
You can add as many validations rules as you like. However, at least one is required.

content.text: Combine Validation rules with AND operator

inlineContent.text: Due to technical limitations, you cannot use
to combine validation rules. Instead, you should use a comma-separated list of validation rules.

code: @Validate(.min(16, message: "you must be at least 16 years old"),
.max(60, message: "you can't be older than 60 years old"))

content.text: Combine Validation rules with OR operator

inlineContent.text: You can use
operator to combine several validation rules.

content.text: Negate Validation rule with NOT operator

inlineContent.text: You can use the
operator to invert a validation rule. The message should reflect the error of the negated validation rule.

content.text: Combine Validation rules with different operator

inlineContent.text: Use AND, OR and NOT operator to create complex validations.
For unsupported types or specific case validations, there is a special validation called
. It allows you to validate a property using a closure.
You can find the full list of validation on the
page. They are listed here by type and protocol conformance.
You can create new validations by extending
. Here’s an example.

code: public extension Validation where Value: Collection {

inlineContent.text: To retrieve the status (
) and the errors list (
), add a
before the validated variable’s name.

code: @Validate(.min(11, message: "you must be at least 11 years old"),
.max(60, message: "you can't be older than 60 years old"))
.maxCount(20, message: "name must be less or equal 20 characters"))
@Validate(.min(16, message: "you must be at least 16 years old"),
.max(60, message: "you can't be older than 60 years old"))
